message:
[high] gmail: pin base_url to googleapis.com hosts

parse_base_url validated scheme and host-presence but accepted any
https host, so a configure payload could redirect Gmail API traffic
to an attacker-controlled server before fcp-google-discovery ran.
The downstream endpoint_allowed_by_policy check only enforced https,
not the host.

- parse_base_url now rejects any non-googleapis.com host unless the
  override targets a local test listener (localhost/127.0.0.1/::1).
  Matches the notion/github/linear host-pinning pattern.
- New host_is_googleapis() uses a proper host-suffix check so
  payloads like "googleapis.com.evil.com" and "evil-googleapis.com"
  are rejected; substring-smuggle payloads like
  "https://evil.com/gmail.googleapis.com/v1" parse to host=evil.com
  and are rejected.
- Updated the existing accept-https test to use gmail.googleapis.com
  and added five new cases: apex/subdomain accept, substring smuggle
  reject, foreign-host reject, googleapis-subdomain accept, and a
  host_is_googleapis truth-table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
